mnemonics.title: CNF Conversion Steps
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'E-U-R-B': Eliminate ε, Unit, Useless, Binary split RHS

mnemonics.title: CNF Definition
mnemonics.content: CNF: 'Two at Most' - at most 2 symbols on RHS, both non-terminals OR single terminal

mnemonics.title: GNF Definition
mnemonics.content: GNF: 'Terminal First' - always start with terminal on RHS

acronyms.usage: All productions A→BC or A→a, used for CYK parsing
All productions A→aα, used for top-down parsing
Grammar where LHS has single non-terminal

visualTips.visualization: Think of CNF as binary tree - each production branches into at most 2 nodes (non-terminals) or is a leaf (terminal)
Think of GNF as linear expansion - each step consumes a terminal then optionally expands non-terminals left-to-right
Flowchart: Original → Remove ε → Remove Unit → Remove Useless → Binary Split → CNF

analogies.analogy: Like breaking a long family tree branch into smaller parent-child pairs to simplify inheritance tracing
Like removing optional chapters from a book - you must account for content before and after the optional part
Like removing a middleman in a supply chain - directly connect producer to consumer
Like a decision tree where each decision starts with seeing a specific input (terminal), then continues
